name: Cinnamon Butterfly Rolls
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 18
In a large bowl, combine sugar, salt, yeast, and 1 cup flour. In a quart saucepan over low heat, heat milk and 1/2 cup butter or margarine (1 stick) until very warm (120-130°F). Butter or margarine does not need to melt. With mixer on low speed, gradually beat liquid into dry ingredients just until blended. Increase speed to medium; beat for 2 minutes. Beat in vanilla extract, 1 egg, and 1 cup flour to make a thick batter; continue beating for 2 minutes, scraping bowl often. With wooden spoon, stir in remaining 2 cups flour to make a soft dough.
Turn dough onto floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes, working in more flour (about 1/3 cup). Shape into ball; place in greased large bowl, turning to grease top. Cover; let rise in warm place (80-85°F) until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
Punch down dough. Turn dough onto lightly floured surface; cut dough in half; cover and let rest for 15 minutes. Grease 2 large cookie sheets. In a small bowl, mix brown sugar, pecans, raisins, and cinnamon. In a small saucepan over low heat, melt 4 tablespoons butter or margarine (1/2 stick).
With floured rolling pin, roll half of dough into a 17 1/2 by 12-inch rectangle. Brush with half of melted butter; sprinkle with half of brown-sugar mixture. From the 17 1/2 inch edge, roll dough in a jelly-roll fashion; pinch seam to seal. Cut roll into 9 wedges, 2-1/2 inches at the wide side and 1 inch at the short side.
Turn wedges short side up. Press handle of wooden spoon across each. Repeat with other half of dough.
Place rolls 2 inches apart on cookie sheets. Cover; let rise in warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 350°F. In a cup, beat remaining egg; with pastry brush, brush rolls. Bake rolls for 20 minutes or until golden. Remove rolls from cookie sheets; cool on wire racks.
